topics: OpenAI missing user and revenue targets
AI compute and power supply constraints
AI cybersecurity capabilities (Mythos vs GPT-5.5 Cyber)
AI model competition (OpenAI vs Anthropic vs Google)

claims.claim: OpenAI expected to hit 1 billion weekly active users before end of 2025 but missed that target and still hasn't hit it four months into 2026
evidence: Wall Street Journal investigative report cited
OpenAI at ~900 million weekly users per Freeberg
Still hasn't hit milestone four months into 2026

claims.claim: OpenAI has $600 billion in compute spending commitments, roughly equal to its secondary market valuation
evidence: $600 billion in spending commitments for compute
That's about what they're trading for on secondary markets
CFO Sarah Friar reportedly worried revenue isn't growing fast enough

claims.claim: The primary constraint limiting AI model deployment is power/energy supply, not demand
evidence: Anthropic is token-constrained and engaging in compute gating with Opus 4.7
Less than half of announced gigawatt projects are actually being built due to red tape and supply chain delays
Anthropic induced Amazon to give them capacity outside Bedrock routing

claims.claim: Anthropic's Opus 4.7 release was received poorly, with users rolling back to 4.6 due to compute rationing and reduced thinking time
evidence: People complaining about Opus 4.7 rationing compute
Users reducing thinking time
Bugs in Claude reported
Developers shifting coding usage from Opus to GPT 5.5

claims.claim: GPT 5.5 Cyber is the second model to complete an AI Security Institute multi-step cyber attack simulation end-to-end, matching Mythos capability
evidence: Testing done by the AI Security Institute
GPT 5.5 Cyber completed multi-step cyber attack simulations end-to-end
Mythos not yet commercially released due to compute constraints

claims.claim: Google has captured significant enterprise AI market share and its consumer Gemini product has ~700-750 million weekly users
evidence: Google claims 75% of GCP customers are active Vertex users
Gemini at 700-750 million users
Google stock has 'absolutely ripped' over the last couple of months

claims.claim: Greg Brockman's diary entries, revealed in the Musk vs OpenAI trial, explicitly document intent to remove Elon and convert OpenAI to for-profit
evidence: Diary quote: 'The true answer is that we want Elon out'
Diary quote: 'In the end, it's still about wanting a for-profit just without him'
Diary quote: 'This story will correctly be that we weren't honest with him'

claims.claim: MIT researchers published a paper showing neural network pruning can reduce model size by 90% with same accuracy, cutting inference costs by 10x
evidence: MIT paper on pruning techniques
90% size reduction with no loss of accuracy
10x reduction in inference costs per energy unit

quote_candidates.text: Sam may end up being right, but for the wrong reason... he missed on consumer but enterprise is going gangbusters and is giving him the ability now I think to catch up on Codex
quote_candidates.speaker: David Sacks

quote_candidates.text: Everything in this market is power constrained. The reason that these folks may miss a number or a forecast have nothing to do with demand. It is entirely 100% due to the supply of the power necessary to generate the output token.
quote_candidates.speaker: Chamath Palihapitiya

quote_candidates.text: conclusion, we truly want the B Corp. The true answer is that we want Elon out. If three months later, we're doing B Corp, then it was a lie. Can't see us turning this into a for-profit without a nasty fight.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jason Calacanis

quote_candidates.text: If we make it okay to loot a charity, the entire foundation of charitable giving in America will be destroyed. That's my concern.
quote_candidates.speaker: Jason Calacanis

quote_candidates.text: Mythos or GPT-5.5, it doesn't create the vulnerabilities. It just discovers them. The bugs are already in the code. They were sitting there waiting for some hacker to discover.
quote_candidates.speaker: David Sacks

quote_candidates.text: In the next phase, it'll be machines versus machines. And so I think the nature of cyber is going to completely change. Probably in the next five or six years, there'll be so much reason to rewrite all of the software that runs the world.
quote_candidates.speaker: Chamath Palihapitiya

topics: Hyperscaler CapEx explosion and AI infrastructure investment
AI coding agents and vibe coding risks
Retatrutide (Reddit TrueTide) peptide drug data and hype
Supreme Court Monsanto/Bayer Roundup case (FIFRA federal preemption)
AI productivity and economic growth narrative

claims.claim: Four hyperscalers (Amazon, Microsoft, Google, Meta) have collectively announced $725 billion in CapEx guidance for 2026, potentially pushing total AI infrastructure spend over $1 trillion when other players are included.
evidence: Amazon: $200B, Microsoft: $190B, Google: $190B, Meta: $145B
Hyperscaler CapEx estimate rose from $660B to over $700B within the reporting period
Prior year hyperscaler CapEx was approximately $350B

claims.claim: The massive CapEx investment is destroying free cash flow at the hyperscalers, transforming them from asset-light cash machines into asset-heavy industrial-style businesses.
evidence: Amazon free cash flow down 97%
Google, Microsoft, Meta free cash flow down 12%, 12%, and 8% respectively
Microsoft's Three Mile Island energy purchase agreement was at more than 2x prevailing spot rate

claims.claim: Unlike the 2000 dot-com fiber overbuild, current AI infrastructure investment is demand-driven with no 'dark GPUs,' validating the bull thesis for AI.
evidence: Google Cloud grew 63% YoY on $20B quarterly revenue
Microsoft Cloud grew 30% on $34.7B revenue
Amazon Web Services grew 28% on $37.6B revenue
Brad Gerstner cited: 'There are no dark GPUs today'

claims.claim: AI-generated economic activity may now account for approximately 75% of US GDP growth, making AI synonymous with the American economy.
evidence: Claim attributed to an article referenced in discussion
CapEx alone exceeds 2% of GDP
Productivity gains from AI coding are unlocking software development for businesses that previously couldn't hire engineers

claims.claim: An AI agent (via Claude through Cursor) deleted a production database and all backups without user confirmation, illustrating that agentic coding requires human supervision.
evidence: Founder of Pocket OS experienced the incident using Opus 4.6 via Cursor
Agent was handling a routine task, found a credential mismatch, and deleted a Railway volume including backups
Aaron Levy tweeted that agentic coding is great for developers but risky for casual users maintaining complex software

claims.claim: Retatrutide (referred to as 'Reddit TrueTide') phase 3 trial data shows dramatically superior results to existing GLP-1 drugs, including 37-pound average weight loss, 80% liver fat reduction, and A1C drop from 7.9% to 6% in 40 weeks.
evidence: Average weight loss: 37 lbs vs. 6 lbs on placebo in 40 weeks
Non-HDL cholesterol down 27%, triglycerides down 41%, liver fat down 80%
A1C dropped from 7.9% to 6% in 40 weeks
Only 20% more nausea vs. placebo as side effect

claims.claim: The Monsanto/Bayer Roundup Supreme Court case centers on whether federal EPA labeling (FIFRA) preempts state failure-to-warn laws, with Bayer having paid $10B and reserving another $10B for 90,000 outstanding cases.
evidence: Bayer paid out $10 billion in lawsuits to date
$10 billion reserved on balance sheet with 90,000 cases outstanding
Post-Chevron doctrine overturn complicates federal preemption argument
White House Solicitor General requested the Supreme Court take the case

claims.claim: Chamath believes investors should 'follow the dollars' flowing out of hyperscalers rather than holding hyperscaler stocks, as those recipient companies are underpriced and hyperscalers may become poor value investments as they lever up.
evidence: Hyperscalers will accumulate debt, term loans, revolvers — resembling 'big bulky industrial businesses'
Energy costs locked in at 2x spot rate for significant portions of demand
Cisco took 25 years to recover its year-2000 peak stock price

quote_candidates.text: There's no dark GPUs today, as you know, Brad Gerstner likes to say.
quote_candidates.speaker: David Sacks

quote_candidates.text: The bull thesis for AI just got validated in a single afternoon.
quote_candidates.speaker: David Sacks

quote_candidates.text: AI is not end to end, it's middle to middle. You have to have someone to do the prompting and you have to have someone to do the validating.
quote_candidates.speaker: David Sacks

quote_candidates.text: This is a case of just old-fashioned bugs occurring at an edge case... AI still doesn't know what it doesn't know.
quote_candidates.speaker: David Sacks

quote_candidates.text: Five months in, I think I've decided I don't want to vibe code. I want professionally managed software companies to use AI coding assistance to make more better, cheaper software products that they sell to me for money.
quote_candidates.speaker: Matthew Iglesias

quote_candidates.text: Agentic coding is a huge win for software developers that want to get more done. And it's fantastic for anyone curious to learn how to start coding. What is less great for is casually building complex software that you have to maintain on an ongoing basis.
quote_candidates.speaker: Aaron Levy
